mission.title: atlantis: intermittent 404 Not Found on /pulls github calls
mission.description: intermittent 404 Not Found on /pulls github calls. This issue affects 21+ users.

steps.title: Identify atlantis troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your atlantis deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review atlantis configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant atlantis configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis
kubectl get configmap -n atlantis -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=atlantis
```
Hi, sometimes when Atlantis is triggered on a PR in github, Atlantis posts the following error onto the PR:

```
Plan Error

GET https://api.github.com/repos/myorg/myrepo/pulls/1163/files?per_page=300: 404 Not Found []
```

Looking at github's API

steps.title: Apply the fix for intermittent 404 Not Found on /pulls github calls
steps.description:
These calls have been 404'ing lately, likely due to eventual consistency
issues on GitHub's side where they send the PR created webhook but it's
not yet available on their API.

To work around this, we will retry up to 3 times with a 1s delay.
```yaml
2020-04-30T10:29:45.155000 [INFO] myorg/myrepo#1163: Creating dir "/home/atlantis/.atlantis/repos/myorg/myrepo/1163/default"
2020-04-30T10:29:45.252000 [INFO] myorg/REPO2#276: Creating dir "/home/atlantis/.atlantis/repos/myorg/REPO2/276/default"
2020-04-30T10:29:45.961000 [INFO] myorg/REPO2#276: Successfully parsed atlantis.yaml file
2020-04-30T10:29:45.963000 [INFO] myorg/REPO2#276: 1 projects are to be planned based on their when_modified config
2020-04-30T10:29:46.152000 [INFO] myorg/myrepo#1163: Successfully parsed atlantis.yaml file
2020-04-30T10:29:46.156000 [INFO] myorg/myrepo#1163: 13 
```

steps.title: Confirm intermittent 404 Not Found on /pulls github calls is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=atlantis -n atlantis --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n atlantis --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
These calls have been 404'ing lately, likely due to eventual consistency
issues on GitHub's side where they send the PR created webhook but it's
not yet available on their API.

To work around this, we will retry up to 3 times with a 1s delay.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with atlantis installed or the issue environment reproducible.
